articles.title: The Race to Recursive Self-improving AI and Exponential Tech
articles.content:
2023年通用人工智能的喧嚣即将转变为2027年的递归人工智能竞赛。递归自我改进的人工智能将从根本上重塑我们体验世界的方式，并在未来几年大幅加速数字化转型。虽然Anthropic长期以来一直声称他们使用Claude进行大部分编码工作，但截至2026年中期，我们还没有任何迹象表明我们已经站在递归自我改进的边缘。然而，令人惊讶的是，大量新的人工智能初创公司声称他们正在将人工智能的递归能力嵌入到芯片设计等高价值行业。他们中的许多人声称这是他们现在的学术重点，其中最近最引人注目的是名为Recursive Superintelligence的公司。你可以想象，一旦人工智能真正擅长于工程改进自身，它将能够相当快速地扩展。但，这将推动科学、研发和创新的新纪元吗？这些热门初创公司在商业上取得成功并以可行方式实现其提议目标的机会有多大？更不用说仅靠生成式人工智能技术了。"从本质上讲，递归自我改进是一种人工智能系统审查、重写和优化自身源代码或架构以变得更智能的过程。"

我相信2027年确实会感觉像是进入了一个与2026年三大人工智能IPO之后完全不同的世界的入口。人工智能生态系统的氛围转变将是切实可感的。DeepSeek的高级管理层已告知其正在进行的人民币700亿元（100亿美元）融资轮的潜在投资者，该公司将把突破性人工智能研究置于短期商业化之上。由于通用人工智能不再是值得认真讨论的商业基准，递归自我改进变成了一个更加具体的命题。人工智能指数级自我改进的想法是技术奇点理论中的一个基本转折点。雷·库兹韦尔在其著作《奇点临近》中——我多年前曾痴迷于此——著名地预测奇点将在2045年发生。现在一些人认为它可能在2030年到2045年之间的任何时候到来。一些人将生成式人工智能能力的快速进步视为它确实即将发生的证据。"今天的系统远未达到通用人工智能。无论你解决多少个埃尔德什问题……我认为它距离真正的发明，或者像拉马努金那样的人所能做到的，还差得很远很远。"——Demis Hassabis，谷歌DeepMind首席执行官，AlphaFold诺贝尔奖得主

背景：Demis Hassabis不仅是谷歌DeepMind的掌门人，原来他还是Anthropic的天使投资人。这是一个紧密交织的影响力网络——DeepMind校友创办的初创公司自2021年以来总共筹集了超过140亿美元。现在OpenAI的校友也在发生同样的情况。Hassabis在Anthropic成为估值数百亿美元的 major player 之前以个人身份进行了早期/天使投资，我估计他可能持有高达20亿美元的个人股份。这些利益冲突在人工智能生态系统中变得越来越普遍，尤其是集中在OpenAI、谷歌DeepMind和Anthropic及其校友周围。人工智能高管越来越多地在私下担任天使投资者。"知情人士表示，Hassabis是Anthropic的天使投资人，后者已成为关键的人工智能竞争对手，也是全球增长最快的初创公司之一。谷歌作为与该公司云和人工智能合作伙伴关系的一部分，已单独向Anthropic投资了数十亿美元。"——《金融时报》

你认为Hassabis对Alphabet本身投资巨额资金于Anthropic并使用其TPU有多大影响？或者与黑石集团合作新建Neo Cloud的决策呢？这些都是循环融资论证中新出现的因素。Anthropic首席执行官Dario Amodei famously创造了人工智能概念"数据中心里的天才之国"来描述未来超越人类专家的人工智能。如果Hassabis是Amodei项目的种子投资者，那么他们似乎站在同一阵线上就说得通了。是IPO前的巧妙营销，还是别的什么？辉煌的科学人工智能未来，还是围绕人工智能研究人员形成的AI贵族阶层的美化作品——他们的言辞大多数时候听起来更像营销叙事而非任何学术性质的东西？考虑到我们与OpenAI的经历，我们必须保持怀疑态度。我们将要押注的是相当投机的东西，但从业界范围来看，从通用人工智能到递归自我改进的转变是切实的，这由2025年末开始的向企业人工智能实用性的行业转向所驱动。这并没有阻止一项能够自动改进自身工作的技术的商业资本吸引力。如果机器中的幽灵确实正在觉醒，那么那些讨厌的入门级工作和职业发展模糊可能只是未来的预兆。在我看来，2027年标志着机器经济时代的开始。个别公司正在通过人工智能代理押注于这种新兴形式，同时却在进行裁员。一批前沿实验室正在将重点放在自我改进的人工智能上，作为驱动主题。风险投资家们似乎认为这是未来的好方法。随着Anthropic证明人工智能编码霸权对企业人工智能产品具有重大经济价值，递归自我改进扩展到不同行业也可能具有巨大的经济价值。最近有人正确地指出，大型科技公司实验室也在多个层面上研究这个。Anthropic聘请Andrej Karpathy可能是递归自我改进的转折点。一个信号表明大型科技公司在2020年代下半年更加认真对待这件事。Andrej Karpathy是一位人工智能研究人员，曾共同创立OpenAI，后来被特斯拉挖走。他表示自己将加入Anthropic。早在2017年，Musk将Karpathy从OpenAI挖走，当时这位特斯拉首席执行官是这两家科技公司的董事会成员。历史上，在OpenAI期间，Karpathy专注于深度学习和计算机视觉，直到2017年离开加入特斯拉。他领导特斯拉的全自动驾驶（FSD）和自动驾驶仪项目，直到2022年离开。然后他回到OpenAI一年，于2024年再次离开，创立了Eureka Labs，这是一家致力于将人工智能助手应用于教育的初创公司。Karpathy是一位著名的斯洛伐克裔加拿大研究人员，曾在Geoffrey Hinton和Fei-Fei Li等顶尖学者的指导下学习人工智能，Karpathy为OpenAI早期GPT的开发做出了贡献。

一些人认为Karpathy加入Anthropic是人工智能竞赛的转折点，甚至可能与Anthropic自身在预训练中对递归自我改进能力的追求有关。使用当前模型（Claude）自动化或优化预训练是迈向更快能力提升和LLM预训练领域早期递归自我改进的经典第一步。这不仅仅是炒作；这正是职位描述所明确指向的。因为构建前沿模型最昂贵、计算密集的阶段，如果其中一部分可以用人工智能自动化，第一个实现这一目标的AI实验室将比竞争对手拥有巨大而显著的竞争优势。我们曾经有专注于通用人工智能的AI初创公司，现在在2026年我们看到更多由递归自我改进驱动的AI初创公司进行巨额融资。这个想法是递归自我学习的人工智能可能成为2020年代最大的研究重点，而不仅仅是人工智能代理能做什么。当Andrej Karpathy宣布他将加入Anthropic的预训练团队，在Nick Joseph手下工作时，Anthropic证实了他的具体目标：建立一个专注于使用Claude加速预训练研究本身的团队。这意味着什么，Anthropic能否成为递归自我学习人工智能的领导者？递归自我学习人工智能的概念最早由数学家I.J. Good在1965年推广，他描述了现在通常所说的智能爆炸。然而，这如何与人类世界相交，如果我们真的看到奇点，以及资本主义、民主、法治和货币等机构如何适应，如果它有意义地发生，还有待观察。很多生成式人工智能已经像黑匣子一样运作，我们很难解释它如何做它所做的事情。这些类似递归自我改进的AI初创公司的最终目标到底是什么，或者它们是否会真正加速创新，还是只是让未来的世界更像它们自己的合成形象，目前还不清楚。如果递归自我改进在AI编码中得到验证，它是否可以仅在其他人工智能pod和相关领域的各种科学、化学、生物技术和工程方面实施？

大量资本最近投入了递归自我改进循环想法。专注于递归人工智能的AI初创公司——围绕自主代码生成、自我改进循环或AI研究代理自动化的系统——以及它们的初始种子轮融资金额：

我会认为这些是我目前雷达上最值得注意的递归自我改进相关AI初创公司，但我相信好的公司将在2027年和2028年到来。

Recursive Superintelligence：初始融资：6.5亿美元（2026年5月完成首轮融资，由GV和Greycroft领投，Nvidia和AMD Ventures跟投）。

Ineffable Intelligence：Ineffable Intelligence获得了Sequoia、Lightspeed、Nvidia和Google的支持，于2026年4月以51亿美元估值从隐形模式走出。他们筹集了11亿美元的种子轮。这家初创公司追求超级智能，由伦敦大学学院教授、前DeepMind强化学习团队负责人David Silver于2025年末创立。

SSI或安全超级智能：初始融资：10亿美元（2024年末筹集，由Sequoia、Andreessen Horowitz和SV Angel支持）。

Meta超级智能实验室（MSL）：Meta自2025年中期推出以来，已承诺直接投入数百亿美元来启动和扩展这个特定部门。我估计2025年和2026年约为250亿美元。虽然不完全是初创公司，但我们必须考虑MSL可能具有的能力，考虑到他们的人工智能人才热潮和计算预算。

Ricursive Intelligence：用于芯片设计的3亿美元A轮融资。Anna Goldie（首席执行官）和Azalia Mirhoseini（首席技术官），前谷歌研究人员，AlphaChip（用于谷歌TPU的AI芯片设计）的共同创造者。

当然，这不是第一个也不是最后一个尝试在芯片设计的各个方面进行递归自我改进的公司。但我认为这是2026年这方面最好的当前例子。

越来越花哨的AI初创公司或新实验室谈论的不是通用人工智能的语言，而是递归自我改进和超级智能。我认为递归自我改进最具影响力的领域包括：

- 大语言模型开发（如预训练）
- 芯片设计
- 机器人设计、自我组装和群体智能
- 代理式AI工作流程
- 先进材料
- 量子计算
- 高级模拟与预测（预测分析）
- AI介导的药物发现和生物技术
- 无人机战争、间谍活动和军事应用
- 能源和太空基础设施技术
- 人类文明殖民（月球、小行星和行星）
- 住房建设和城市规划及工业干预
- 建造人类-AI共生殖民飞船
- 应对文明范围挑战的解决方案，如全球变暖、人口老龄化和生育危机

就Anthropic而言，使用当今最好的模型来改进明天模型的训练是迈向更快能力提升和LLM预训练领域早期递归自我改进的经典第一步。AI实验室正在思考如何加速研发，并使企业能够解锁新能力，特别是围绕成本效率方面。自2025年以来，我们看到更多专注于科学问题本身的AI初创公司横跨各个领域和行业，用新工具包应对熟悉挑战。随着通用人工智能炒作退居幕后——Anthropic可能在2026年超越OpenAI然后上市——以及Anysphere（Cursor）的年收入增长超越其竞争对手，很明显企业人工智能在不久的将来将具有递归自我改进动态。Tokenmaxing等趋势在2026年初至中期推动了Anthropic和Cursor等公司的年收入增长，随着大型人工智能三巨头的IPO在未来几周发生，这应该会更快增长。



2027年可能是人工智能热潮的峰值，这与2022年底以来形成的势头有关。但递归自我学习人工智能系统真正发展成果的种子可能正在酝酿之中（这与通用人工智能不同）。如果2026年是前沿企业在公司和企业级人工智能解决方案中实施代理式人工智能的关键年份，那么这种技术需要多长时间才能以普遍方式向普通大众普及？消费者和普通纳税人将付出什么代价？2027年的阶跃函数变化或类似Mythos的模型将带来什么新能力，使RSI更接近现实？华尔街预计，到2028年，超大规模云服务商在人工智能资本支出上的投入每年将超过1万亿美元。但黄仁勋认为，到2030年，人工智能基础设施总支出可能达到4万亿美元（他早在2025年秋季就提出这一观点）。英伟达首席财务官科莱特·克雷斯确实提供了时间表，称人工智能基础设施年度支出预计在2030年之前达到3至4万亿美元。人工智能初创公司获得数百亿美元融资，但人工智能基础设施、GPU、数据中心和能源基础设施的投资则高达数万亿美元。黑石首席执行官拉里·芬克承认，用于建设数据中心和电网的数万亿美元将来自普通人的储蓄账户和养老基金，并称这是必须的。他说，美国需要数万亿美元的人工智能基础设施支出，人们将被强制“投资”于此。“其中大部分将来自储蓄账户和养老账户。”

芬克指出，目前有数万亿美元闲置在传统低收益银行账户和货币市场基金中（仅美国就约25万亿美元）。美国持有的国债不断增长，这意味着为美国亿万富翁从中获利而实现人工智能未来的窗口期可能正在缩小。但消费者已经在为此付出代价——税收增加、电费上涨、当地社区建设数据中心、互联网体验恶化。K型经济意味着金融精英可能愿意牺牲一半消费者的利益来实现他们的愿景和目标。传统上，汽车贷款和抵押贷款可能紧随其后。在这个世界上，美国前十大股票现在占整个标普500指数的41%创纪录高位。半导体股票的的交易价格比其200日移动均线高出60%，这是自1999年泡沫以来的最大差距（Easy Markets）。我们愿意在种子人工智能上投入多少，即使它可能阻碍而非帮助我们的人类发展？递归超级智能是一家于2025年底成立的前沿人工智能初创公司。这家总部位于伦敦和旧金山的人工智能初创公司从隐秘状态中现身，宣布了一轮6.5亿美元的种子和风险投资，估值达46.5亿美元。对于一个没有产品、未经证实的团队来说，这不是一个小型融资轮。这家备受瞩目的人工智能初创公司的许多联合创始人实际上是中国人。理查德·索彻（You.com）只是名义上的负责人。中国恰逢其时地走向成熟，而RSI正接近早期阶段的实际实现。美国人没有人愿意承认这一点，或者他们的能源优势以及更集中的人工智能人才。该公司的人工智能将通过在“开放式的自动化科学发现过程中”进行模拟来寻找自我改进的方法。让人想起日本的Sakana实验室。联合创始人（七到八人）包括：蒂姆·罗克塔舍尔（前谷歌DeepMind/UCL）、乔希·托宾、杰夫·克鲁恩、石天明（前OpenAI）、田元东（前Meta）、熊才明，以及其他人。团队拥有来自OpenAI、谷歌、Meta、DeepMind等公司的专业知识。田元东，前Meta FAIR（基础人工智能研究）团队研究科学家总监，与其他七位联合创始人共同创立了递归超级智能。“我们的主要重点是在规模上构建真正的递归、自我改进的超级智能，这意味着研究想法的构思、实现和验证的整个过程将是自动化的。”

我们的联合创始人之一蒂姆·罗克塔舍尔曾领导谷歌DeepMind的开放性和自我改进团队，特别参与了世界模型Genie 3的工作，这是开放性的一个很好的例子。根据递归超级智能的说法，其人工智能模型进行的实验将专注于改进其代码以及测试工具。更多的Neo Labs（专注于RSI和AGI的人工智能实验室）将在2027年至2032年期间成立，因为人工智能基础设施继续加速增长。中国的深度求索获得融资也意味着它属于同一阵营。SpaceX人工智能也可能成为SpaceX从IPO获得的资金（约750亿美元）的受益者。“我们在旧金山和伦敦的25名顶尖研究人员和工程师带来了多元化专业知识，涵盖代理式人工智能科学家、架构和算法设计、世界模型、优化和可解释性，我们共同的信念是，这是我们今天能够致力于的最重要的问题。”——来源。在通往RSI的竞赛中，硅谷有一个相当标准的观点，递归超级智能的领导者似乎也认同：“关于递归自我改进的想法之一是，一旦我们有了这种系统，计算就成为唯一重要的资源。你运行系统的速度越快，它改进的速度就越快，外部人类活动不会真正产生影响。”——来源。递归超级智能是一家罕见的获得谷歌和英伟达双方支持的AI初创公司。包括来自GV（谷歌 Ventures）、Greycroft、英伟达、AMD等公司的投资。这段视频是一周前的：在他们的网站上，这家初创公司坚持认为：“递归超级智能认同这一逻辑结论：实现超级智能的最快途径将通过递归自我改进的人工智能来实现，并通过推动无尽创新的开放式算法实现。”

如果SpaceX正在尝试解决轨道计算并有望成为重要的Neo云，那么它是否也与RSI竞赛有关？对于企业应用来说，什么将比RSI更大的阶跃函数？SpaceX特斯拉最终可能成为一家能源公司和Neo云，不确定他们指的是什么企业级人工智能应用。2027年SpaceX的大部分收入将来自为Anthropic等公司提供Neo云服务。埃隆·马斯克的xAI在2025年仅凭32亿美元的收入就亏损了64亿美元。人工智能部门资本支出从2025年的127亿美元攀升至2026年第一季度仅一个季度就达77亿美元。SpaceX人工智能增长艰难：根据文件，截至2026年3月，SpaceX的Grok人工智能功能月活跃用户（MAU）为1.17亿，而Grok和X的合并MAU总计5.5亿。这意味着只有五分之一的合并生态系统用户活跃使用Grok人工智能功能。理查德·索彻于1983年出生于德国，在斯坦福大学师从人工智能先驱吴恩达和自然语言处理权威克里斯托弗·曼宁学习，2014年完成博士论文，并获得当年斯坦福计算机科学系最佳博士论文奖。看到如此多的资金流向种子公司，你可以感受到2026年人工智能热潮中的狂热，这是人工智能历史上前所未有的。随着许多人工智能初创公司、人工智能芯片公司和机器人公司在过去六个月在中国进行IPO，这些紧密联系的生态系统中对人工智能的未来以及可能出现的未来持有一些乐观态度。随着超大规模云服务商资本支出的增加，风险投资的押注也在相应增加，以支付GPU、计算和人工智能人才费用。这不是押注人工智能超越人类，而是押注人工智能以自动化方式不断改进。但是，你需要多少个Neo Labs和多少计算能力才能达到RSI，更不用说AGI了？没有人知道。点击此处阅读我的AGI系列更多文章。感谢阅读！
articles.summary:
The antics of AGI of 2023 is going to turn into the race to recursive AI (RSI) of 2027. Recursive self-improving AI could fundamentally reshape how we experience the world and fundamentally accelerate digital transformation in the years to come.

While Anthropic has been claiming they use Claude for the majority of their coding since quite a while, there are no signs that we are on the cup of RSI just yet in mid 2026.

However there are a surprising number of new AI startups that claim they are building recursive capabilities of AI into super valuable industries, like chip design. Many of them claim this is their academic focus now and the startup called Recursive Superintelligence really comes to mind most recently. You can imagine how AI engineering their own improvements can scale fairly quickly once and if it gets good at this.

But will this propel a new era of science, R&D and innovation broadly speaking? And what are the chances of these buzzy startups at being commercially successful and actually succeeding in a commercially viable way at what they propose will soon be possible? Let alone with just Generative AI technology.

“At its core, recursive self-improvement (RSI) is a process where an artificial intelligence system reviews, rewrites, and optimizes its own source code or architecture to become smarter.”

  • I believe 2027 will indeed feel like the entry point into a fundamentally different world post the three major AI IPOs of 2026. The vibe-shift in the AI ecosystem is going to be tangible.

  • DeepSeek’s senior management has told potential investors in its ongoing 70 billion yuan ($10 billion) funding round that the startup will prioritize groundbreaking AI research over short-term commercialization.

  • With AGI not a commercial benchmark worth talking about seriously, RSI becomes a much more concrete proposition. The idea of AI improving itself exponentially is a fundamental inflection point in the theory around the Technological singularity. Ray Kurzweil, in his book The Singularity is Near, (that I used to obsessed over many years ago) famously predicted that the singularity will occur by 2045. Now some believe that it could arrive anywhere between the years 2030 and 2045. Some see the rapid improvements of Generative AI’s capabilities as evidence it is indeed on the way to happening.

From AGI to RSI Goals for the Future of AI

"Today's systems are nowhere near [AGI]. Doesn't matter how many Erdős problems you solve… I think it's far, far from what a true invention, or someone like Ramanujan, would have been able to do." - Demis Hassabis, CEO of Google DeepMind and Nobel laureate for AlphaFold

Context: Demis Hassabis isn’t just Google DeepMind’s Czar, it turns out he’s an angel investor in Anthropic. It’s a tightly woven interconnected web of influence - where the network of startups spun out by DeepMind alumni have collectively raised a collective $14 billion plus since 2021.

Now the same is occurring with OpenAI alum.

Hassabis invested personally in the early/angel stage, before Anthropic became a major player valued in the tens or hundreds of billions, and I’d estimate could have up to a $2 Billion personal stake. These financial conflicts of interest are becoming quite common in the AI ecosystem especially clustered around OpenAI, Google DeepMind and Anthropic and their Alum. AI executives increasingly are angel investors on the side.

“People familiar with the matter said Hassabis was an angel investor in Anthropic, which has since become a key AI rival and among the world’s fastest-growing start-ups. Google has separately invested billions of dollars in Anthropic as part of its cloud and AI partnership with the company.” - The FT

How much of an impact was Hassabis on Alphabet itself investing huge sums into Anthropic and using their TPUs do you suppose? Or decisions to partner with Blackstone on new Neo Cloud do you suppose? These are newly available factors in the circular financing argument.

Manufactured Geniuses hidden in the awesome demand for Compute?

Anthropic's CEO Dario Amodei famously coined the AI concept "a country of geniuses in a data center" to describe future AI that surpasses human experts. If Hassabis is a seed investor in Amodei’s project, it makes sense why they seem on the same page. Clever marketing before an IPO, or something else?

A glorious scientific AI future or a glamorized work of the AI Aristocracy that appears to be forming around AI researchers whose rhetoric feels more like a marketing narrative than anything remotely academic on most days. Considering what we’ve been through with OpenAI, we have got to be skeptical. We are going to be betting on something fairly speculative to say the least but the switch from AGI to RSI is tangible driven by an industry wide pivot to Enterprise AI utility that started in late 2025.

Why RSI is going to become a Race

That doesn’t stop the commercial capitalistic appeal of a technology that could potentially be capable of improving upon its own work automatically. If the ghosts in the machine are awakening indeed those pesky entry level jobs and career ladder ambiguity might just be a sign of things to come.

In my view, 2027 marks the beginning of the Machine Economy era. Individual companies are betting on a nascent for of this with AI agents already while doing human layoffs. A cluster of frontier labs are pushing the emphasis on self-improving AI now as the driving theme. Venture Capitalists appears on board that this is a great approach for the future.

With Anthropic demonstrating that AI coding supremacy has significant economic value for Enterprise AI offering, RSI spun out into different sectors could have enormous economic value as well.

Recently rightly pointed out that BigTech Labs are also looking into this on many levels. The Anthropic hiring of Andrej Karpathy is potentially an inflection point in RSI. A signal that BigTech is taking is way more seriously in the second half of the 2020s.

  • Andrej Karpathy, an artificial intelligence researcher who co-founded OpenAI before getting poached by Tesla, said he’s joining Anthropic.

  • Back in 2017, Musk recruited Karpathy away from OpenAI while the Tesla CEO was a board member at both tech companies.

  • Historically while at OpenAI, Karpathy focused on deep learning and computer vision until he departed in 2017 to join Tesla. He led Tesla’s Full Self-Driving (FSD) and Autopilot programs before leaving in 2022. He then went back to OpenAI for one year before leaving again in 2024 to start Eureka Labs, a startup dedicated to applying AI assistants to education.

  • Karpathy is famous and historically is a slovakian-born Canadian researcher who studied A.I. under leading academics such as Geoffrey Hinton and Fei-Fei Li, Karpathy contributed to the development of OpenAI’s early GPT

Is Automating some aspects of LLM Pretraining Possible?

Some see Karpathy joining Anthropic as a turning point in the AI race and possibly even related to Anthropic’s own pursuit of RSI like capabilities in pre-training.

Automating or optimizing it (Pretraining at Anthropic) with the current model (Claude) is a direct step toward recursive/self-improving training loops (often called RSI or “autoresearch”). This isn’t just hype; it’s explicitly what the role description points to.

Because its the most expensive, compute-intensive phases of building a frontier model if part of it can be automated with AI, and the first AI labs to accomplish this would have a great and significant competitive edge over their rivals.

View his YouTube

The Setup for the Singularity

We used to have AGI focused AI startups, now in 2026 we are seeing more huge funding raises by RSI focused AI startups. The idea is that recursive self-learning AI might be the biggest research focus of the 2020s and not just what AI agents can do. When Andrej Karpathy announced he was joining Anthropic's pre-training team under Nick Joseph, Anthropic confirmed his specific objective: building a team focused on using Claude to accelerate pre-training research itself. What does this mean, could Anthropic become a leader in recursive self-learning AI?

A Mysterious “Inflection Point” may be Near

The concept of recursive self-learning AI was first popularized by mathematician I.J. Good in 1965, who described what is now commonly referred to as an intelligence explosion. However how that intersects with the human world, if we really see a singularity and how institutions like capitalism, democracy, rule of law and money adapt if it meaningfully takes place remains to be seen.

Much of Generative AI already operates like a black box where we struggling to explain how it does what it does. It’s not clear what the end-game of these RSI like AI startups is exactly or if they will meaningfully accelerates innovation or just make the next world more in their own synthetic image.

If RSI is proven in AI coding, can it just be implemented in other AI pods and labs related to various aspects of science, chemistry, biotechnology and engineering?

Advent of RSI AI Startups ✨

💵 A lot of capital has recently gone into RSI loop ideas.

AI startups focusing on recursive AI—systems built around autonomous code generation, self-improving loops, or agentic automation of AI research—along with their initial seed round funding amounts:

I would consider these among the most notable RSI related AI startups right now on my radar, but I believe the good ones will arrive in 2027 and 2028. 📡

  • Recursive Superintelligence: Initial Funding: $650 million (First funding round closed in May 2026, led by GV and Greycroft, with backing from Nvidia and AMD Ventures).

  • Ineffable Intelligence: Ineffable Intelligence garnered backing from Sequoia, Lightspeed, Nvidia and Google, and emerged from stealth with a $5.1 billion valuation in April, 2026. They raised a $1.1 Billion seed round. The startup is pursuing superintelligence and was founded in late 2025 by UCL professor and former lead of DeepMind’s reinforcement learning team, David Silver. Website link.

  • SSI or Safe Superintelligence: Initial Funding: $1 billion (Raised in late 2024, backed by Sequoia, Andreessen Horowitz, and SV Angel). Website link.

  • Meta Superintelligence Labs (MSL): Meta has committed tens of billions of dollars directly to kick-start and scale this specific division since launching it in mid-2025. I would estimate around $25 Billion in 2025 and 2026. Historical link. While not a startup exactly, we have to consider what MSL might be capable of considering their AI talent binge and compute budget.

  • Ricursive Intelligence: $300 million Series A for chip design. Anna Goldie (CEO) and Azalia Mirhoseini (CTO), former Google researchers and co-creators of AlphaChip (AI for chip design used in Google’s TPUs). Website link. Of course it’s not the first or the last trying to do RSI for various aspects of chip design. But I think it’s the best current example of this in 2026. Learn more.

Increasingly the most flashy AI startups or Neo Labs talk not in a language of AGI, but RSI and Superintelligence.

Areas where I consider RSI to be most impactful include:

  • Large language model development (e.g. Pretraining)

  • Chip Design

  • Robot design, self-assembly and swarm intelligence

  • Agentic AI workflows

  • Advanced materials

  • Quantum computing

  • Advanced simulations & predictions (predictive analytics)

  • AI mediated drug discovery and biotechnology

  • Drone warfare, espionage and military applications

  • Energy and Space Infrastructure Technologies

  • Human civilization colonization (of Moons, asteroids and planets)

  • Housing construction & urban planning and industrial interventions

  • Building human-AI symbiotic colony ships

Tacking solutions to civilization wide challenges like global warming, aging populations and the fertility crisis

Sparks of RSI

As far as Anthropic goes, using today’s best model to improve the training of tomorrow’s model is the classic early step on the path to faster capability gains and perhaps early-stage RSI in LLM pretraining as a field.

AI labs are thinking about how to speed up R&D and enable Enterprises to unlock new capabilities improving faster especially around cost efficiency. Since 2025 we’ve seen more AI startups focused on scientific problems itself across fields and industries tackingling familiar challenges with new toolkits.

As AGI hype fades to the background as Anthropic is likely to overtake OpenAI in 2026 before they go public, and as Anysphere’s (Cursor) ARR surges ahead its clear Enterprise AI will have RSI dynamics in the near future.

2026 is a big year for ARR Increases by Anthropic, OpenAI and Cursor (Anysphere)

Trends like tokenmaxing have surged the ARR of companies like Anthropic and Cursor in early to mid 2026 and this should increase even faster as the BigAI three IPOs occur in the weeks ahead.

2027 is the likely peak of the AI boom as far as the momentum created in late 2022 is concerned. But there’s a probability that seeds of real developments in recursive self-learning AI systems could be in the making. (not the same as AGI).

If 2026 is a key year in ARR surge at companies at the frontier of implementing Agentic AI in companies and Enterprise (AI) solutions, how long does it take to diffuse into the general population in a ubiquitous way? And what will be the cost to consumers and the average taxpayers?

What will the step-function change in 2027 or new capabilities of models like Mythos that brings RSI that much closer?

Building the Body of Recursive self-learning AI or aka Superintelligence?

Wall Street expects hyperscalers to spend more than $1T annually on AI CapEx by 2028. But Jensen Huang believes total AI infrastructure spending could reach $4T by 2030. (He was first saying this in the Fall of 2025). Nvidia’s CFO Colette Kress has indeed provided a timeline, stating that the annual expenditure on AI infrastructure is expected to reach $3 to $4 trillion before 2030.

  • Hundreds of Billions to AI startups in funding

  • But Trillions in AI Infrastructure, GPUs, Datacenters and Energy infrastructure.

Who gets to Pay for it?

The CEO of BlackRock, Larry Fink, admits that the trillions of dollars being used to build data centers and power grids will come from ordinary people’s savings accounts and pension funds, and says it is mandatory.

He says America needs trillions in AI infrastructure spending, and that people will be forced to “invest” in it.

“Much of this will come from savings accounts and pension accounts.”

Fink pointed out that trillions of dollars are currently sitting idle in traditional, low-yield bank accounts and money market funds (roughly $25 trillion in the U.S. alone).

The growing National debt the U.S. possesses means there might be a shrinking window to manifest the future of AI for American Billionaires to profit from it. But consumers are already paying for this in taxes, higher electricity bills, datacenters in their location communities, a worsening internet experience.

Credit Card Delinquencies are Rising in the U.S. during an AI Stock Market Boom

The K-Shaped economy means the financial elite are likely willing to sacrifice the bottom half of consumers to achieve their vision and goals. Traditionally auto loans and mortgages could come next.

  • A world where the top 10 US stocks now represent a record 41% of the entire S&P 500.

  • Semiconductor Stocks are trading 60% above their 200-day moving average, the largest margin since the 1999 Bubble (Easy Markets).

Case Study: Recursive Superintelligence

How much are we willing to invest in the Seed AI that might hinder more than it helps our humanity?

Recursive Superintelligence is a frontier AI startup founded in late 2025. The London and San Francisco-based AI startup, emerged from stealth to announce a massive $650M seed and venture funding round, valuing the company at $4.65 billion. This is not a small round for an unproven team with no product.

Read Article by CEO

Many of the Co-founders of this celebrated AI startup are actually Chinese. Richard Socher (You.com) is just the figure-head. China has the timing to mature just as RSI becomes closer to pragmatic early-stage realization. Nobody in America likes to admit this, or their energy abundance and more dense AI talent concentration.

The company’s AI will search for ways to improve itself by carrying out simulations “in an open-ended process of automated scientific discovery.” Reminiscent of Sakana Labs in Japan.

The Seven Co-Founders of Recursive Superintelligence

Co-founders (seven or eight): Include prominent researchers like Tim Rocktäschel (ex-Google DeepMind/UCL), Josh Tobin, Jeff Clune, Tim Shi (ex-OpenAI), Yuandong Tian (ex-Meta), Caiming Xiong, and others. The team has expertise from OpenAI, Google, Meta, DeepMind, and more.

Tian Yuandong, former research scientist director at Meta’s FAIR (Fundamental AI Research) team, launched Recursive Superintelligence alongside seven other co-founders.

Automated Research Labs: the goal:

“Our main focus is to build truly recursive, self-improving superintelligence at scale, which means that the entire process of ideation, implementation, and validation of research ideas would be automatic.”

Tim Rocktäschel, one of our co-founders, led the open-endedness and self-improvement teams at Google DeepMind and particularly worked on the world model Genie 3, which is a great example of open-endedness.

According to Recursive, the experiments carried out by its AI model will focus on improving not only its code but also its harness.

More Neo Labs (RSI and AGI focused AI labs) will be forming in especially the 2027 to 2032 window as AI infra continues to ramp. DeepSeeking getting funding in China also means it is in the same group. SpaceX AI could also be a beneficiary of the money SpaceX gets from its IPO (around $75 Billion).

“Our 25 top researchers and engineers in San Francisco and London bring diverse expertise spanning agentic AI scientists, architecture and algorithm design, world models, optimization, and interpretability, united by a shared conviction that this is the most important problem we could be working on today.” - Source.

Compute, Energy, HBM Memory Chips, GPUs and Resources are Seen as the Bottleneck to the Speed

In the race to RSI Silicon Valley has a pretty standard view that the leaders of Recursive Superintelligence seem to adhere to:

“One of the ideas around recursive self-improvement is that, once we have this sort of system, compute becomes the only important resource. The faster you run the system, the faster it will improve, and there’s no outside human activity that will really make a difference.” - Source.

“Recursive self-improving superintelligence to automate knowledge discovery.”

Recursive Superintelligence is a rare AI startup to attract backers from both Google and Nvidia. Including funding from GV (Google Ventures), Greycroft, Nvidia, AMD, and others. This video was one week ago:

$4 Billion Valuation in Seed Round

On their website the startup insists that “Recursive embraces the logical conclusion: the fastest path to superintelligence will be realized by AI that recursively improves itself, and does so via open-ended algorithms that drive endless innovation.”

If SpaceX is trying to solve orbital compute and on pace to become an important Neo Cloud, is it also connected to the race to RSI? What will be a bigger step-up order function than RSI for Enterprise applications?

The Proposed TAM of SpaceX in its S-1 feels AI Generated

SpaceX Tesla will likely end up as an Energy company and Neo Cloud, not sure what Enterprise AI applications they are referring to.

SpaceX faces Debt, Cash Burn and Revenue issues

The majority of SpaceX's revenue in 2027 will come from being a Neo Cloud to companies like Anthropic.

  • Elon Musk’s xAI lost $6.4 billion from operations on just $3.2 billion in revenue in 2025.

  • AI segment capital expenditures climbed from $12.7 billion in 2025 to $7.7 billion in the first quarter of 2026 alone.

  • SpaceX AI is struggling to grow: Per the filing, SpaceX recorded 117 million monthly active users (MAUs) for Grok AI features as of March 2026, out of 550 million total MAUs across Grok and X combined. That implies only one-fifth of the combined ecosystem is actively using Grok AI features.

Unicorn Seed AI Neo Labs ⚗️

Richard Socher was born in Germany in 1983, studied under AI pioneer Andrew Ng and NLP authority Christopher Manning at Stanford University, completed his doctoral thesis in 2014, and won the best doctoral thesis award in the Stanford Computer Science Department that year. Seeing this amount of funding going to Seed companies you can feel the euphoria in the AI boom in 2026 like never before in the history of AI.

With many AI startups, AI chip firms and robotics companies going IPO in China as well in the last six months, there’s some optimism in these tightly knit ecosystems about the future of AI and what may be on the horizon.

As the hyperscaler Capex rises, the Venture Capital Bets are rising in proportion to pay for the GPUs, compute and AI talent. It’s not a bet on AI that surpases people, it’s a bet on AI that keeps improving in an automated way.

Seed Rounds for Neo Labs Keep Getting Bigger 2026

But how many Neo Labs and how much compute do you need to reach RSI, much less AGI? Nobody knows.

Read more articles in my AGI series here.

Thanks for reading!
